source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design a crew-accessible stowage rack for a deep-space habitat stowage benchmark as a multi-part bolted rack assembly consisting of the 6061-T6 extruded corner-post frame, the honeycomb sandwich side walls (left + right), the honeycomb sandwich back wall, the honeycomb sandwich top and bottom panels, two pivoting quarter-turn-latched doors, three internal shelves dividing the four CTB bays, and the four corner-post hard-dock bolts interfacing to the habitat standoff. Rack outer envelope 1050 mm H x 530 mm W x 813 mm D, compliant with International Standard Payload Rack (ISPR) footprint; it must host four 406 mm x 330 mm x 254 mm cargo transfer bags (CTBs), each fully loaded to 27.2 kg per NASA-STD-3001. Rack hosts two pivoting crew-facing doors latched by paired Nomex-reinforced quarter-turn fasteners. Material: Aluminum 2024-T3 honeycomb panel (skin E = 73 GPa, face-sheet thickness 0.8 mm, core 25 mm Al honeycomb density 32 kg/m^3 with out-of-plane compression 1.5 MPa and shear 0.85 MPa, total panel areal density 5 kg/m^2) for walls + shelves; frame uses Al 6061-T6 extrusion. Environment: launched in SLS Block 1 cargo section under 9 g axial + 2 g lateral quasi-static + random vibration per NASA-HDBK-7005 spacecraft PSD, operated in-hab at 1 atm / 22 C nominal. Load cases: (LC1) launch quasi-static: 4 x 27.2 kg = 108.8 kg CTB mass inside rack + 35 kg rack mass, apply 9 g axial + 2 g lateral simultaneous; (LC2) random vib qual per NASA-HDBK-7005 (6.9 Grms from 20-2000 Hz, 2 min/axis); (LC3) crew operational load: 300 N vertical on an open door (crew stabilizing); (LC4) ECLSS hose routing reaction: 150 N lateral on the routing clip at top of rack; (LC5) thermal: rack surfaces 10-40 C nominal, during de-stow 5 C temporary exposure at exterior door; (LC6) hard-dock preload: frame bolts into habitat standoff at four corner posts with 5 kN preload each. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in frame Al 6061-T6 under LC1 <= 184 MPa (yield 276 / 1.5); (R2) honeycomb panel face-sheet strain under LC1 <= 0.3%; (R3) core shear under LC1 <= 0.57 MPa (0.85 / 1.5); (R4) random-vib 3-sigma stress <= 184 MPa; (R5) first natural frequency of loaded rack above 35 Hz to stay above primary launch vehicle excitation band; (R6) rack dry mass <= 35 kg. Verification is performed on the assembled frame + sandwich panels + doors + shelves model with representative bolted/bonded interface stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, quasi-static FEA, random-vib analysis, modal (10 modes), thermal FEA, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a crew-accessible stowage rack for a deep-space habitat stowage benchmark as a multi-part bolted rack assembly (6061-T6 corner-post frame + honeycomb sandwich walls + pivoting doors + internal shelves + hard-dock bolts).

Outer envelope 1050 mm H x 530 mm W x 813 mm D (ISPR footprint), hosting four 406 x 330 x 254 mm CTBs at 27.2 kg each per NASA-STD-3001. Panels are Al 2024-T3 honeycomb (skin E 73 GPa, 0.8 mm faces, 25 mm core at 32 kg/m^3 with 1.5 MPa out-of-plane compression and 0.85 MPa shear, areal density 5 kg/m^2); frame Al 6061-T6 extrusion. Environment: SLS Block 1 cargo, in-hab at 1 atm / 22 C nominal.

Load cases.
- LC1 launch quasi-static: (4 x 27.2 kg = 108.8 kg CTB + 35 kg rack) at 9 g axial + 2 g lateral simultaneous.
- LC2 random vib qual per NASA-HDBK-7005 (6.9 Grms from 20-2000 Hz, 2 min/axis).
- LC3 crew stabilize: 300 N vertical on an open door.
- LC4 ECLSS hose: 150 N lateral at routing clip on top of rack.
- LC5 thermal: 10-40 C nominal, 5 C temporary exterior exposure during de-stow.
- LC6 hard-dock: four corner-post bolts at 5 kN preload each.

First natural frequency of loaded rack > 35 Hz; rack dry mass <= 35 kg.

prompt.title: XHab stowage rack (multi-part bolted assembly)
prompt.brief: ISPR-footprint stowage rack: 6061-T6 corner-post frame + 2 side walls + back wall + top + bottom + 2 doors + 3 shelves + 4 hard-dock bolts, for four 27.2 kg CTBs.

parts.name: corner_post_frame
parts.role: 6061-T6 extruded four-corner-post frame forming the rack primary structure

parts.name: side_wall_panel
parts.role: left / right honeycomb sandwich side wall panel

parts.name: top_bottom_panel
parts.role: honeycomb sandwich top and bottom closing panels

parts.name: internal_shelf
parts.role: horizontal shelf dividing the four CTB bays

parts.name: hard_dock_bolt
parts.role: corner-post bolt attaching rack to habitat standoff at 5 kN preload

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 1050 mm H x 530 mm W x 813 mm D ISPR footprint

boundary_conditions.interfaces: honeycomb sandwich panels bolted + bonded to extruded corner-post frame; doors hinged + quarter-turn latched to frame; internal shelves bolted into side walls; hard-dock bolts preloaded at four corner posts with representative bolt stiffness
boundary_conditions.support: four corner-post hard-dock bolts fixed to habitat standoff in LC1-LC6

prompt.objective: minimize dry mass under 35 kg while passing yield, face-sheet strain, core shear, random-vib, and modal gates

interfaces.load_path: side wall panels bolted + bonded into corner-post frame
back wall panel bolted + bonded into corner-post frame
top and bottom panels bolted + bonded into corner-post frame
crew doors hinged + latched to corner-post frame
internal shelves bolted into side wall panels
hard-dock bolts preloaded through corner-post frame into habitat standoff

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: ISPR-footprint stowage racks combine an extruded corner-post frame with bonded/bolted honeycomb sandwich walls, doors, shelves, and hard-dock bolts; launch 9g quasi-static and random-vib load paths explicitly cross frame/panel/door interfaces and must be modelled as a multi-body rack.
notes.speculation_caveats: Shelf count, door latch layout, and hard-dock bolt grade are speculative; materials, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
